reason: Both answers land the same core recommendation (take it, conditional on use-of-funds/career-path fit) and cover dilution, default-alive framing, and the decision test well. A adds a nice concrete negotiation/leverage tip (shop the term sheet, push price), but also overstates that $2M "extends runway indefinitely," which is imprecise. B is more precise on the growth math, treats the "roughly default-alive" status with appropriate nuance (14 months isn't a huge margin), and more substantively engages the "terms beyond valuation" point by noting how liquidation preference affects a mediocre exit outcome — something A only gestures at ("seed terms are solid") without examining. Overall comparable quality with a slight edge to B for precision and deeper treatment of deal terms.
